critical_invariants: PCIe configuration space protocol: address = 0xCF8, data = 0xCFC port I/O must not change
BAR allocation must respect size alignment requirements (BAR size = power of 2)
PCIe ECAM (Enhanced Configuration Access Mechanism) memory-mapped offsets are hardware-defined
Virtio device discovery must check vendor ID 0x1AF4 and device ID ranges
PCI capability list traversal must follow next-pointer chain correctly
Bus/Device/Function (BDF) encoding in config address is fixed by PCI spec
MSI/MSI-X capability structure offsets are defined by PCI specification
known_opportunities: Bus scan optimization: cache discovered devices instead of re-scanning
Skip known-empty bus/device/function combinations after initial probe
Batch PCI config space reads using 32-bit reads instead of multiple 8/16-bit reads
Early-exit bus scan when no device found at function 0 (non-multifunction device)
Parallel bus scanning across independent bus numbers
ECAM (memory-mapped) config access instead of legacy CF8/CFC port I/O where available
